heading: Donation of vessels in the National Defense Reserve Fleet
label: 57103 Donation of vessels in the National Defense Reserve Fleet

children.text: is a non-profit organization, a State, or a municipal corporation or political subdivision of a State;
agrees not to use, or allow others to use, the vessel for commercial transportation purposes;
agrees to make the vessel available to the Government whenever the Secretary indicates that it is needed by the Government;

agrees to hold the Government harmless for any claims arising from exposure to asbestos, polychlorinated biphenyls, lead paint, or other hazardous substances after conveyance of the vessel, except for claims arising from use of the vessel by the Government;
has a conveyance plan and a business plan that describes the intended use of the vessel, each of which has been submitted to and approved by the Secretary;
has provided proof, as determined by the Secretary, of resources sufficient to accomplish the transfer, necessary repairs and modifications, and initiation of the intended use of the vessel; and

children.label: A
children.text: the recipient will, at the discretion of the Secretary, reconvey the vessel to the Government in good condition except for ordinary wear and tear; or

children.label: i
children.text: the recipient shall distribute the vessel, as an asset of the recipient, to a person that has been determined exempt from taxation under section 501(c)(3) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 (), or to the Federal Government or a State or local government for a public purpose; and26 U.S.C. 501(c)(3)

children.label: ii
children.text: the vessel shall be disposed of by a court of competent jurisdiction of the county in which the principal office of the recipient is located, for such purposes as the court shall determine, or to such organizations as the court shall determine are organized exclusively for public purposes.

children.heading: .—Other Equipment
children.label: b .—Other Equipment
children.text: At the Secretary’s discretion, additional equipment from other obsolete vessels of the Fleet may be conveyed to assist the recipient with maintenance, repairs, or modifications.

children.heading: .—Additional Terms
children.label: c .—Additional Terms
children.text: The Secretary may require any additional terms the Secretary considers appropriate.

children.heading: .—Delivery of Vessel
children.label: d .—Delivery of Vessel
children.text: If conveyance is made under this section, the vessel shall be delivered to the recipient at a time and place to be determined by the Secretary. The vessel shall be conveyed in an “as is” condition.

children.heading: .—Limitations
children.label: e .—Limitations
children.text: If at any time prior to delivery of the vessel to the recipient, the Secretary determines that a different disposition of the vessel would better serve the interests of the Government, the Secretary shall pursue the more favorable disposition of the obsolete vessel and shall not be liable for any damages that may result from an intended recipient’s reliance upon a proposed transfer.

children.heading: .—Reversion
children.label: f .—Reversion
children.text: The Secretary shall include in any conveyance under this section terms under which all right, title, and interest conveyed by the Secretary shall revert to the Government if the Secretary determines the vessel has been used other than as described in the business plan required under subsection (a)(5).
